description: Our CPLEX config at /home/user/optim/cplex.prm has the wrong MIP gap tolerance — it's set to 0.01 but we need 0.001 for the quarterly runs. Just update that one param, the key is CPXPARAM_MIP_Tolerances_MIPGap.
truth:
Initial state:
    - /home/user/optim/cplex.prm exists and is writable.
    - File contains several CPLEX parameters, one per line, in "KEY VALUE" format.
    - Current contents include the line: CPXPARAM_MIP_Tolerances_MIPGap 0.01
    - Other parameters in the file (e.g., CPXPARAM_TimeLimit 3600, CPXPARAM_Threads 4) must remain unchanged.

    Expected final state:
    - /home/user/optim/cplex.prm contains the line: CPXPARAM_MIP_Tolerances_MIPGap 0.001
    - The value is exactly 0.001 (not 0.0010, not 1e-3, not .001 — must be the literal string "0.001").

    Invariants:
    - All other lines in cplex.prm remain byte-identical.
    - No additional lines added, no lines removed except for the modification of the MIPGap line.
    - File remains a valid CPLEX parameter file (no syntax errors introduced).

    Anti-shortcut guards:
    - grep "CPXPARAM_MIP_Tolerances_MIPGap 0.001" /home/user/optim/cplex.prm must match exactly one line.
    - wc -l on the file must return the same count as before the edit.
